title: Dimension Mnemonics
text: Dimension Mnemonics The dimension control mnemonics define the various dimension modes the device supports, for example, portrait, landscape, condensed print, A3, and A4 format. The current mode_ is activated, after init , at the start of a session and when the display is refreshed. Arguments Mnemonic Explanation mode_ n ( x , y )[ w , d ] Specify the dimensions for mode n , where n is a number form 0 through 7. Parameters x and y —define the dimensions of the mode in characters and lines, for example mode_0(80,24) . A definition for mode_0 is required for all device definitions. [ w , d ] —optional; define the resolution of one character cell in graphics mode, that is, the width and depth of each cell in pixels. The default is [1,1] . Uniface uses this information to scale positioning requests when in graphics mode. Description Up to eight different modes are supported by Uniface. Table 1. Modes for VT Device Translation Tables (Except VT340G) Mode Explanation 0 Normal screen dimension, 80 columns, 24 lines. 1 Wide screen dimension, 132 columns, 24 lines. 4 Print layout for 80 columns, 66 lines. 5 Print layout for 132 columns, 66 lines. 6 Print layout for 80 columns, 72 lines. 7 Print layout for 80 columns, 72 lines. Undefined modes are, by default, mapped to an existing mode. mode_0 is mandatory and must therefore be defined. If mode_1 is not defined, the settings for mode_0 are used. Even-numbered modes which are not defined use the same settings as mode_0 . Odd-numbered modes which are not defined use the same settings as mode_1 . The comments column with the mode_ instructions is shown to the users, when they browse the Device Mode entry on the Define Print Jon Model form. It is good practice to define comments with all defined modes. The following example shows the mode_ definitions used for a standard VT display: ^027[?3l ^MODE_0(80,24) normal screen dimension ^027[?3h ^MODE_1(132,24) wide screen dimension ^027[?3l ^MODE_2(80,24) normal screen dimension ^027[?3h ^MODE_3(132,24) wide screen dimension ^027[?3l ^MODE_4(80,66) print layout 66 lines ^027[?3h ^MODE_5(132,66) print layout 66 lines ^027[?3l ^MODE_6(80,72) print layout 72 lines ^027[?3h ^MODE_7(132,72) print layout 72 lines Copy The following example shows the mode_ definitions used for a standard LN03 printer: ^027[6^032J^027[11m^027[0w^013 ^MODE_0(80,66) portrait, pitch 10.3 ^027[6^032J^027[11m^027[2w^013 ^MODE_1(96,66) portrait, pitch 12 ^027[6^032J^027[11m^027[3w^013 ^MODE_2(105,66) portrait, pitch 13.2 ^027[6^032J^027[11m^027[2w^013 ^MODE_3(96,6) portrait, pitch 12 ^027[6^032J^027[15m^027[9w^013 ^MODE_4(120,88) small portrait, pitch 15 ^027[6^032J^027[15m^027[4w^013 ^MODE_5(132,88) small portrait, pitch 16.5 ^027[7^032J^027[15m^027[0w^013 ^MODE_6(152,60) landscape, pitch 13.6 ^027[7^032J^027[15m^027[4w^013 ^MODE_7(184,60) landscape, pitch 16.5 Copy Related Links Parameters in the Output Sequence Some mnemonics include parameters in the output sequence that can be used to determine layout and formatting information, such as horizontal and vertical coordinates, numeric base zero (decimal, octal, or hexadecimal), zero padding, and so on. The output sequence can even include expressions that use the parameters incalculation, for example, to increment values. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
